channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Muturi Alleges Government Pressure on Nairobi Hospital Board
item.description: Nairobi — The Democratic Party leader, Justin Muturi, has publicly accused President William Ruto of trying to take control of Nairobi Hospital through intimidation and pressure on its board. Accordin...

item.title: Alberta Landowners Block Oil Company Over Unpaid Lease Fees
item.description: Some landowners in southwest Edmonton are taking direct action against MAGA Energy, an oil company that has reportedly failed to pay its lease for three years. Mark Dorin and Dale Braun erected a wood...

item.title: Trump Shifts Stance on High Oil Prices Amid Iran War and Strait of Hormuz Disruptions
item.description: President Donald Trump, who previously highlighted low gasoline prices—around $2.30 per gallon in his February 2026 State of the Union address—has changed his rhetoric following the U.S.-initiated war...

item.title: Residents of Phugwani Village Still Face Water Shortages Despite Promises of Borehole
item.description: Residents of Phugwani village near Malamulele in Limpopo are still struggling with water shortages after being promised a borehole before the 2024 elections. According to locals, no municipal water ta...

item.title: Jets struggle defensively in 6-3 defeat to Rangers, playoff hopes diminish
item.description: The Winnipeg Jets suffered another disappointing defeat, losing 6-3 to the New York Rangers on Thursday night. After hoping to rebound from one of their worst performances this season, the Jets quickl...

item.title: Twelve House of Reps Members Officially Join APC and ADC
item.description: Twelve members of Nigeria's House of Representatives don officially leave their former parties — PDP, Labour Party (LP), and Young Progressives Party (YPP) — and join the ruling All Progressives Congr...

item.title: PCO Implements Energy-Saving Measures with 4-Day Workweek
item.description: The Presidential Communications Office (PCO) is implementing new energy-saving measures as part of President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr.'s directive to conserve energy amidst rising oil prices. A ...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Issues First Statement as Iran's Supreme Leader Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: Iran has released the first public statement in the name of its newly appointed supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, signalling the continuation of military operations against US assets and the strategic...

item.title: Pakistan highlights challenges in Iran nuclear issue amid Security Council divisions
item.description: On Thursday, Pakistan informed the UN Security Council that worsening diplomacy in the Middle East has made the Iran nuclear issue even more complex. Pakistan's Permanent Representative, Asim Iftikhar...

item.title: Tanzanian Businesses Face Disruptions Due to Middle East Conflict
item.description: Eleven days after the US and Israel started military action against Iran, Tanzanian businesses are beginning to feel the consequences. Traders in major markets like Kariakoo are reporting delays in sh...

item.title: The Arrest and Legacy of the Knights Templar on 13 October 1307
item.description: The Knights Templar, founded on Christmas Day 1119, were a unique order of warrior monks committed to protecting pilgrims in the Kingdom of Jerusalem. Contrary to popular portrayals in modern cinema, ...

item.title: Gazini Ganados represents Philippines in first-ever MGI All Stars pageant
item.description: Filipino beauty queen Gazini Ganados is once again stepping onto the international pageant scene as she joins her fellow Philippine representatives in the inaugural Miss Grand International (MGI) All ...

item.title: LPG Shortage and Oil Price Concerns Amid West Asia Conflict
item.description: On March 13, 2026, oil prices remained around $100 per barrel as tensions escalated in West Asia, with Iran’s leader threatening to block the strategic Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil shipping rout...

item.title: Heavy rains cause deaths, flooding, and disruption across Tanzania
item.description: Heavy rains have been pounding many parts of Tanzania, causing severe damage and loss of life. In Dodoma Region alone, five people died when floods swept them away or walls collapsed on them in Kondoa...

item.title: Senator Gatchalian calls for phased increase in electricity rates to protect consumers
item.description: Senator Sherwin Gatchalian has urged the Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) to implement electricity rate hikes gradually, citing the expected impact of ongoing tensions in the Middle East on power co...

item.title: Former Nairobi Police Officer Talaam Granted Hospital Treatment During Murder Trial
item.description: Nairobi — The court has okayed that former Nairobi Central Police Station OCS Samson Talaam, who is charged in connection with the death of Albert Ojwang, be taken to Kenyatta National Hospital for sp...

item.title: Tanzanian court refers Burundi oil supply dispute to London arbitration
item.description: Dar es Salaam High Court has paused proceedings in a commercial dispute between Burundi’s Prestige Investment SA and UAE’s Lamar Commodity Trading DMCC, ordering that the matter go for international a...

item.title: Assam CM Himanta Sarma Plans to Repost Symbolic Video Targeting Undocumented Immigrants
item.description: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has announced his intention to repost a controversial video in which two Muslim men were symbolically depicted as being shot. The video, initially posted by th...

item.title: DCP Anthony Placid Officially Becomes Nigeria Police Force Public Relations Officer
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Olatunji Disu, don approve DCP Anthony Okon Placid as di new Force Public Relations Officer (FPRO), wey go replace ACP Benjamin Hundeyin. DCP Placid na very expe...

item.title: Pakistan to Begin 5G Pilot in Major Cities as 4G Services Improve
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan is all set to take a big step in its digital journey as IT Minister Shaza Fatima announced that 5G pilot services will commence next week in major cities. Speaking at a ceremony fo...
